name: ai-note-draft
title: AI Note Draft
description: Editable, sectioned note draft with an original-versus-generated comparison slot and deliberate accept, edit, discard, and copy actions.

props.name: sections
props.description: { id, heading, content, edited?, placeholder?, provenance?, readOnly? }. Controlled — the component holds no text of its own.

props.name: sections[].edited
props.description: Marks the section as changed by the reviewer. Caller-owned, so the indicator cannot claim an edit the caller never recorded.

props.name: sections[].provenance
props.description: Per-section sources — AiCitation elements or an AiEvidencePanel.

props.name: status
props.description: Position in the review flow. Only the caller advances it; the component never accepts on its own.

props.name: editing
props.description: Content is read-only until this is true. Controlled.

props.name: onEditingChange
props.description: Omit to remove the edit control.

props.name: comparison
props.description: Original-versus-generated view — a diff, two columns, whatever the caller has. Rendered above the sections.

props.name: onAccept
props.description: Caller writes to the record. Omit to hide the accept control entirely — for example, without permission.

props.name: onDiscard
props.description: Discards the draft. Must not touch the underlying record.

props.name: confirmAccept
props.description: Accept is a two-step confirmation, and is disabled while editing.

props.name: extraActions
props.description: Extra footer controls, after the built-in review actions.
metadata, confidence, limitations, and evidence pass through to AiAnswerCard; actions is owned by this component.

clinicalNotes: A note is a legal record, and the failure mode this component exists to prevent is generated prose sliding into it without anyone deciding that it should. So the draft is inert by default: text is read-only until the reviewer explicitly enters edit mode, the draft is labelled "not filed", and a standing line under the content says it is not part of the record. Accepting is deliberate and separated from reading — accept is disabled while editing (the text you accept is the text that is filed), and it takes a second, confirming click, so a mis-click cannot document a patient. `onAccept` is caller-owned: this component never writes, and a caller who omits it has no accept button at all, which is how you model a user without documentation rights. Discard is non-destructive — it drops the draft and leaves the underlying record untouched — and the discarded state says so, because a reviewer must never wonder whether rejecting a draft deleted a real note. Edits are surfaced rather than absorbed: a section the reviewer changed is marked, and the whole draft shows an edited indicator, so a later reader can see that the filed text is no longer what the model produced. Provenance is per-section, and the comparison slot exists so the reviewer can see the draft against what the record already says before choosing to overwrite it.
notes: Composes AiAnswerCard (owns its actions slot; pass extras via extraActions). Fully controlled: sections, editing, and status all live with the caller. Section variants exported as aiNoteDraftSectionVariants.
